helpful: The complete petition and extracted questions presented made the asserted legal theory and absence of a claimed circuit split clear.
The docket snapshot exposed selection signals such as the requested response, amici, and procedural timing.
CourtListener supplied the lower-court majority's vehicle facts and related post-Bonta disclosure decisions.
The fee-class and originating-circuit base rates supported quantitative calibration.
gaps: No brief in opposition was available by the snapshot date, leaving the respondent's cert-stage framing unobserved.
notes: The provisioned record was sufficient for a completed forward prediction. Live legal research was useful for testing whether the petition presented a developed circuit conflict; no corpus query was needed because its structured filters would not target this legal issue.
